name: Charlie Barnet
abstract: Charles Daly Barnet (October 26, 1913 – September 4, 1991) was an American jazz saxophonist, composer, and bandleader. His major recordings were "Nagasaki", "Skyliner", "Cherokee", "The Wrong Idea", "Scotch and Soda", "In a Mizz", and "Southland Shuffle".

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Charlie Barnet in the context of Woody Herman's influences; the interviewee (Sam Jones) does not respond or provide any personal connection to Charlie Barnet.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I took a leave of absence my last year of Juilliard to go on the road with Barnet. I started off on fourth tenor and then on the second tour I was playing lead alto.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Charlie Barnet’s band... I went out to California to join Charlie Barnet... Gerald says, 'Charlie, this is your new trumpet player.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: He was on Stan Kenton’s band and Charlie Barnet, all the bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'listen, would you like to work with Charlie Barnet’s band?' I said, 'get out of here, give me that again, for real?' ... 'Okay you’ve got it.' ... Now we’re at the Strand
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: My association with Charlie Barnet was 1940. The last of '39 and '40 -- and he gave me all the freedom in the world... I wanted Charlie Barnet who just said play
